variables.description: Whether the constitution requires a jury or any form of citizen participation in decision-making in criminal trials (includes people's assessors, court assessors, jurors)
Whether there is a special mention of victims' rights in the constitution
Whether the constitution regulates the collection of evidence (includes search and seizure regulation and exclusion of involuntary confessions)
Whether the constitution provides for the right or possibility of pre-trial release (includes bail provisions)

Whether the constitution provides for the right to protection from unjustified restraint (habeas corpus). Includes prohibition of arbitrary detention and requirements for formal accusation or warrant.

Whether the constitution mentions nulla poena sine lege — the principle that no person should be punished without law. Distinct from ex post facto provisions; includes arrest, detention, and punishment.
Whether defendants have an explicit right to appeal judicial decisions
Whether the constitution requires that the names of those imprisoned be entered in a public registry
How the constitution treats the use of capital punishment
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Universally Prohibited; 2=Prohibited Except in the Case of War; 3=Explicitly Allowed; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: How the constitution treats the use of corporal punishment (deliberate infliction of pain as correction or punishment)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Universally Prohibited; 2=Prohibited Except in the Case of War; 3=Explicitly Allowed; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution explicitly mentions due process (must be explicitly stated, not implied)
Whether the constitution provides for the right to examine evidence or confront all witnesses
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Evidence only; 2=Witnesses only; 3=Both; 4=Neither; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution prohibits punishment by laws enacted ex post facto (retroactive criminal laws)
Whether the constitution provides for some right of redress in the case of false imprisonment, arrest, or judicial error
Whether the constitution provides the right to a fair trial (includes 'honest trial' or right to 'impartial justice')
Whether the constitution provides for the right to a speedy trial (includes 'within reasonable time')
Whether the constitution generally requires public trials
Whether there is a presumption of innocence in trials
Whether the constitution specifies the trial must be conducted in a language the accused understands, or guarantees the right to an interpreter
Whether the constitution gives juveniles special rights or status in the criminal justice process (includes separate detention from adults or limits on punishments for minors)
Whether the constitution provides for the prohibition of double jeopardy (being tried for the same crime twice)
Whether the constitution gives the accused a right to silence or protection from self-incrimination
Whether the constitution provides the right to counsel if one is indicted or arrested (includes right to defense)
Whether counsel is provided at the state's expense (asked only if COUNS=1)
